records.ety: [From F. hausser to lift, raise (cf. OF. haussere towpath, towing, F. haussire hawser), LL. altiare, fr. L. altus high. See Haughty.]

defs: A large rope made of three strands each containing many yarns.
notes: ☞ Three hawsers twisted together make a cable; but it nautical usage the distinction between cable and hawser is often one of size rather than of manufacture.
